philosophy: Extreme diversification achieved through factor-neutral, autonomously managed sub-pods. Each PM runs an isolated book targeting idiosyncratic alpha. Center risk management prevents correlated blowups through aggressive, non-negotiable drawdown enforcement.
gross_net_style: +/- 2% Strict Net Bounds — violations trigger immediate forced rebalancing

factor_exposure.beta: Zero — hard neutralized at portfolio level
factor_exposure.momentum: Neutralized — factor exposure models run continuously
factor_exposure.idiosyncratic: Very High — all alpha must be stock-specific

sector_concentration: Hard cap at 5% per sector — enforced daily by risk systems

risk_constraints.forced_liquidation_trigger: PM Drawdown > 3% triggers 50% gross cut; > 5% triggers full termination

preferred_instruments: Sector ETF Hedges (XLF, XLK, XLE, XLV)

historical_regime_behavior.q4_2018_rate_hike: Center risk cut PM books aggressively as factor correlations converged to 1.0 near the December lows — systematically sold quality at the bottom.
historical_regime_behavior.march_2020_covid: Survived via immediate gross deleveraging (book cut from 200% to 80% in 5 sessions). Missed the V-shape recovery entirely as risk limits kept teams out.
historical_regime_behavior.q3_2022_inflation: Forced sector neutralization via XLF shorts as rates sector outperformed uncontrollably. Underperformed peers who maintained directional bets.

macro_reaction_function: Drawdown survival mechanics override all fundamental judgment. When macro shocks force correlation convergence, center risk systems mechanically cut PM allocations regardless of underlying asset value — systematically reinforcing downward momentum in liquid names.
confidence_notes: Very High Confidence. Pod center risk architecture is inflexible by design. Liquidation triggers are mathematical and fully automated. Behavior under stress is highly reproducible.
